name: Personal Agent Action Router
tagline: A human-aware queue for personal agent actions
thesis: Personal Agent Action Router turns recordings, translations, and human context into queued actions that can be checked against timezone, sleep, family, persona, and approval rules before any tool executes. It gives a personal AI system a practical middle layer between intent and action: Kafka-style event buffering, policy gates, tool adapters, and an observer log that makes every outcome replayable.
audience: People building personal agents that need to act through tools without interrupting the human at the wrong time or crossing identity, approval, or family-time boundaries.
proofPoints: Context intake combines attendant recording, AI translation, and persona memory into a structured action candidate.
The queue layer separates action creation from execution so policy checks, retries, and audit trails stay explicit.
The constraint layer blocks or defers actions around timezone, sleep, family time, approval needs, and tool permissions.

launchChecks.label: Bind the simulator to real recorder, translation, and queue events
Add Zapier and Replit adapters behind explicit approval receipts
